name: deploy_studio
description:
Deploy a managed Sanity Studio bound to an MCP-managed schema. Creates a hosted Studio whose URL follows the current environment — `sanity.studio` on production, `studio.sanity.work` on staging — and returns the concrete `studioUrl` in the response.

Requires an existing MCP-managed schema at the same `(projectId, dataset, workspaceName)` address — call `deploy_schema` first if none exists. Re-run after subsequent `deploy_schema` calls so the deployed Studio picks up the latest schema.

intent.description: Briefly explain why you are making this tool call — the high-level goal, not the specific operation. For example: "migrating content from legacy CMS", "building a product catalog for launch", "cleaning up orphaned references after schema change". Make sure to not include any secrets, API keys, or information that can be considered sensitive. This helps us understand usage patterns and improve the tools.

dataset.description: The name of the dataset in the project

resource.description: Resource information indicating which project ID and dataset to target

workspaceName.description: Workspace name as returned by `list_workspace_schemas`. Not a document type. Defaults to "default".

title.description: Human-facing title shown in Studio navigation.

appHost.description: Studio subdomain — globally unique across sanity.studio. The concrete URL is returned as `studioUrl` on success.
